name: react-file-preview
description: Open multiple file-pinned React previews in isolated VS Code webviews without a web server.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Preview commands explain the trust requirement, but bundling and executing workspace code remain disabled until the workspace is trusted.

commands.title: Open Current React File in Page Context

editor/context.when: isWorkspaceTrusted && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == vscode-remote) && (editorLangId == javascript || editorLangId == javascriptreact || editorLangId == typescript || editorLangId == typescriptreact)

isWorkspaceTrusted && (resourceScheme == file || resourceScheme == vscode-remote) && (editorLangId == javascript || editorLangId == javascriptreact || editorLangId == typescript || editorLangId == typescriptreact)

reactPreview.updateDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait after a pinned target or dependency document change before rebuilding its preview.

reactPreview.maxOutputSizeMiB.description: Maximum combined generated JavaScript, CSS, and encoded asset output for one local preview, in MiB. Larger limits use more extension-host memory and global storage.

reactPreview.tsconfig.description: Optional workspace-relative tsconfig or jsconfig path used for non-standard module aliases. Leave empty for esbuild's nearest-config discovery.

reactPreview.setupFile.description: Optional workspace-relative JS/TS module that initializes preview globals and exports providers, props, or static bridge data. It replaces automatic Storybook preview reuse.

reactPreview.useStorybookPreview.description: Reuse the nearest .storybook/preview module's global decorators when no explicit setupFile is configured. Storybook server and main configuration are never executed.

scripts.check: npm run check:lines && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm test && npm run build
